event: Jesus beneath the winepress — the Mystical Winepress
symbols: Christ in the winepress — His body crushed like grapes, His blood poured out as wine for the salvation of the world
Red-stained garments — the blood of the Passion, fulfilling Isaiah's imagery of the one who treads the winepress
Latin inscriptions — liturgical texts connecting this image to the Eucharistic sacrifice
Chalice below — the cup of the new covenant in Christ's blood
description: A fresco depicts Christ in a dynamic kneeling or crouching position, wearing red-stained garments draped around his torso and legs. His body appears beneath the mechanism of a winepress. Latin inscriptions in banners surround the figure. A vessel or chalice is visible at the bottom. The palette is dominated by reds and warm tones against a pale background. The figure's pose conveys both effort and suffering.
theological_description: The Mystical Winepress (Torcular Christi) is a powerful allegorical subject that fuses Isaiah's vision of the one who treads the winepress (ISA.63.1-3) with the Eucharistic theology of Christ's blood. Isaiah asks: 'Why is your apparel red, and your garments like his who treads in the winepress?' The divine answer: 'I have trodden the winepress alone, and from the peoples no one was with me.' The Church Fathers applied this text to Christ's Passion — He alone bore the weight of sin, His blood pressed out like wine for the redemption of the world. The image directly connects to Christ's words at the Last Supper: 'This cup is the new covenant in my blood, which is poured out for you' (LUK.22.20). The winepress becomes a visual theology of the Eucharist: Christ's suffering produces the wine of salvation. Revelation echoes this imagery: 'He treads the winepress of the fury of the wrath of God the Almighty' (REV.19.15).

scripture_refs.reason: Who is this with garments stained crimson, treading the winepress alone — the direct source of this imagery
This cup is the new covenant in my blood — the Eucharistic connection to Christ's pressed blood
He treads the winepress of the fury of God — the eschatological echo of Isaiah's winepress
I am the true vine — Christ as the source of the wine of salvation
This is my blood of the covenant, poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins
